lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] It's 23:00 Madison time. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
If you're listening, you've made a choice. [pause] That choice is still valid.
Let's begin with breath. In through the nose. [pause] Four counts.
Hold. [pause] One. Two. Three. Four.
Out through the mouth. [pause] Slowly.
Again. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
Notice where you are right now. The room. The light. The sounds you can hear.
Notice the sounds you're *not* hearing. [pause] That silence is a choice too.
Scan your body. Start at the crown. Tension?
Release it. [pause] Let it fall away.
Down to the shoulders. Are you holding yourself small tonight? [pause] You can stop.
The chest. The heart. Is it racing? [pause] That's information. Not judgment.
Breathe into the fear. [pause] You're still here. You're safe.
The hands. Release what you're gripping. [pause] Nothing outside that door is yours to hold.
The belly. Breathe there. [pause] That's where the truth lives.
And the legs. The feet. [pause] You are rooted.
Now. [pause] I want you to think about silence.
There's a kind of silence that comes when everyone stops pretending. [pause] When the masks come off.
On this night, the silence is different. [pause] Isn't it?
Most years, the noise tells you what's happening. [pause] Tonight, the quiet is the message.
The East Side is usually loud by now. [pause] But something's different.
The violence has patterns. [pause] Tonight, the pattern breaks.
This is not a meditation on comfort. [pause] I need you to understand that.
This is a meditation on what it means to be the ones inside. [pause] While the night unfolds outside.
Breathe. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
Picture your door. [pause] The one that separates you from the night.
It's closed. [pause] You closed it. That was an act of will.
An act of love, maybe. [pause] For yourself. For the people inside.
Do you feel guilty about that? [pause] Don't.
Guilt is a luxury. [pause] Survival is not.
Now picture the people you decided not to save tonight. [pause] Everyone outside that door.
Not because you don't care. [pause] But because you can't.
The ones doing the hurting. The ones being hurt. [pause] None of them are your responsibility.
Repeat that. [pause] Not. Your. Responsibility.
Feel the weight lift. [pause] Even if it's small.
Even if it comes back after this hour ends. [pause] For now, it's gone.
There's a kind of prayer that isn't words. [pause] It's just breath.
It's just the sound of someone choosing to stay alive. [pause] To stay sane.
Sha-lo-va-nim. [pause] That means nothing. [pause] But it means something.
The Sisters taught me that. [pause] That sometimes the sound is the prayer.
Continue breathing. [pause] The sound is working.
{{ELAPSED}} have passed since this began. [pause] You've survived all of them.
The night is long. [pause] But you are longer.
When dawn comes — and it will come — you will still be here.
The ones outside will either come back through transformation. [pause] Or they won't come back.
That's the Purge. [pause] It purges. It defines. It takes inventory.
And you — [pause] you are part of the count too.
The ones who stayed. [pause] The ones who chose the door.
Breathe. [pause] In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
When you leave this space, the world will still be waiting. [pause] That's not your fault.
Stay with us. [pause] Stay inside. [pause] Stay breathing.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative at 23:00 mark: 82 | East Side reports unusual quiet pattern after 22:15
"The silence is the prayer. The breathing is the answer."
WEATHER UPDATE: Cold front arrival accelerating. Temperature dropping. Rain beginning.
Willy Street barricade at Yahara Bridge holding. 15+ defenders. Zero interior casualties. Neighborhood watch status: secure.
